buggy_code:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge

X = pd.read_csv('factors.csv', index_col='date').values
y = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', index_col='date').values.ravel()

# PCA fitted on ALL data
pca = PCA(n_components=5)
X_pca = pca.fit_transform(X)

# Split after PCA transformation
X_train, X_test = X_pca[:700], X_pca[700:]
y_train, y_test = y[:700], y[700:]

model = Ridge()
model.fit(X_train, y_train)
preds = model.predict(X_test)
clean_code:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline

X = pd.read_csv('factors.csv', index_col='date').values
y = pd.read_csv('returns.csv', index_col='date').values.ravel()

# Split BEFORE PCA
X_train, X_test = X[:700], X[700:]
y_train, y_test = y[:700], y[700:]

# PCA fitted on training data only
pipeline = Pipeline([
    ('pca', PCA(n_components=5)),
    ('model', Ridge())
])
pipeline.fit(X_train, y_train)
preds = pipeline.predict(X_test)
description: PCA dimensionality reduction fitted on entire dataset before train/test split.
explanation: PCA computes principal components from the covariance structure of all data. When fitted on the full dataset, the components capture variance from test-set observations. The transformed test features are therefore influenced by test-set statistics, creating data leakage.
suggested_fix: Fit PCA only on training data. Use sklearn Pipeline to enforce this automatically.

issues.title: PCA fitted on full dataset leaks test set information
